Caches the existence of SEND_TEXT_NOTIF_PREF_KEY and provides functions to initialize and check support.

# Purpose
This header file defines a simple interface for a text notification service. It includes the declaration of two functions: [`send_text_service_init`](<#send_text_service_init>) and [`send_text_service_is_send_text_supported`](<#send_text_service_is_send_text_supported>). The [`send_text_service_init`](<#send_text_service_init>) function initializes the text service, while [`send_text_service_is_send_text_supported`](<#send_text_service_is_send_text_supported>) checks if sending text notifications is supported. The file also serves as a cache for the existence of the `SEND_TEXT_NOTIF_PREF_KEY` and a reply action within that key, which helps to avoid performance issues by reducing flash memory reads when determining if the app should be shown in the launcher.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### send\_text\_service\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:send_text_service_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/send_text_service.h#L27>)

Initializes the send text service.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize the send text service, which prepares the system to handle send text notifications and actions. It must be called before any other operations related to send text services to ensure proper setup and event handling. This function subscribes to necessary event updates, enabling the system to respond to relevant changes.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`send_text_service_init`](<send_text_service.c.md#send_text_service_init>)  (Implementation)


---
### send\_text\_service\_is\_send\_text\_supported<!-- {{#callable_declaration:send_text_service_is_send_text_supported}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/send_text_service.h#L29>)

Checks if sending text is supported.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine if the system supports sending text messages. It checks both the system capabilities and a specific reply action. Call this function when you need to verify text sending support before attempting to send a text message. Ensure that the system capabilities are initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns `true` if sending text is supported; otherwise, returns `false`.
- **See Also**: [`send_text_service_is_send_text_supported`](<send_text_service.c.md#send_text_service_is_send_text_supported>)  (Implementation)
